prompt: Rolling restarts of the exporter take 10+ minutes and k8s ends up SIGKILLing the pods. From the logs, the process manager cancels every tenant consumer and then sits there: tenants with an empty queue shut down instantly, but any tenant with a backlog keeps exporting job after job as if nobody said stop — Run only comes back once the whole backlog is drained or the pod gets killed mid-write (which is how we corrupted Friday's export). Shutdown is supposed to be: cancel, finish the job in hand, return. It reproduces here: `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` fails. Fix consumer.go; consumer_test.go must not change.
